steps.title: Pin down what critics praised
steps.content:
The passage says critics praised Tsang for “creatively transforming a museum rotunda” using projection and sound. The praise is specifically about her original, creative approach to the space — not about restraint, mystery, or inexplicability.
  • Choice A — 'restrained': Suggests holding back or limiting — the opposite of creatively transforming a space. ✗
  • Choice B — 'inventive': Means original and creative, which matches exactly what critics praised: her creative transformation of the rotunda. ✓
  • Choice C — 'inexplicable': Means impossible to explain — a negative connotation inconsistent with critics praising her specific creative choices. ✗
  • Choice D — 'mystifying': Means confusing or baffling — again inconsistent with praise directed at deliberate, creative decision-making. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.

steps.title: Confirm the word fits the blank
steps.content:
The blank describes Tsang's approach as recognized by critics. “Inventive” (Choice B) means showing creativity and original thinking, which directly echoes the passage's “creatively transforming.” No other choice carries a positive connotation of originality.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
